- Fix issue where adding definition to an empty consolidated definition file results in an invalid definition file

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Refactored the consolidated file parser to use a LL parser format.

While this release has been tested for regression issues, there may be a possibility that some of your definition files may face issues. If so, ensure that your definition file adheres strictly to the specification in the README. If you're certain there is a bug with the parser, please raise an issue.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Add definition context:
### Usage

To easily add context to your note:
1. Use the `Add definition context` command
2. Search and select your desired context

You can do this multiple times to add multiple contexts.

### How it works

`Add definition context` adds to the _properties_ of your note.
Specifically, it adds to the `def-context` property, which is a `List` type containing a list of file paths corresponding to the selected definition files.
In source, it will look something like this:
```
---
def-context:
	- definitions/def1.md
	- definitions/def2.md
---
```

You can edit your properties directly, although for convenience, it is recommended to use the `Add definition context` command to add contexts as it is easy to get file paths wrong.

### Removing contexts

To remove contexts, simply remove the file path from the `def-context` property.
Or if you want to remove all contexts, you can delete the `def-context` property altogether.

- Tag definition files and folders in file explorer
- Disabled free-text input for definition folder settings
- Allow registration of definition folder in file menu (right click on folder within file explorer)

### Bug fixes
